title: CDSS
subtitle: Clinical Decision Support System
description: AI-powered clinical decision support that assists physicians with evidence-based recommendations, differential diagnosis, and treatment guidance at the point of care.

title: EkaScribe
subtitle: AI Medical Scribe
description: Ambient AI scribe that records doctor-patient conversations and automatically generates structured clinical notes, SOAP notes, and prescriptions in 20+ languages including English and regional Indian languages.

title: DocAssist
subtitle: Clinical AI for Doctors
description: Clinical AI assistant combining voice-powered documentation, intelligent patient profile management, and real-time clinical insights. Features drug interaction alerts, multi-language support, and automated SOAP note generation.

title: Document Understanding
subtitle: Medical Document AI
description: Automates parsing of unstructured medical documents — prescriptions, lab reports, insurance claims — with handwritten OCR, PII redaction, and SNOMED-CT / LOINC coding support.

title: Heart Rate Monitor
subtitle: Camera-based vitals
description: Measures heart rate using the smartphone's back camera via photoplethysmography — no additional equipment needed. Useful for fitness tracking and detecting when medical consultation may be necessary.

title: Saraga
subtitle: Open datasets for Indian art music
description: Large-scale open dataset for research on Carnatic and Hindustani music, featuring multitrack audio, melodic annotations, and metadata. Published in Empirical Musicology Review 2021.

title: Riyaz
subtitle: AI music practice app
description: AI-powered practice app for Indian classical music providing real-time feedback on pitch accuracy, tala adherence, and raga grammar. Co-founded MusicMuni Labs to build and commercialize this. 1M+ installs.

title: PhD Research
subtitle: Melodic description in Indian art music
description: Doctoral research on computational approaches for melodic description in Indian classical music corpora — melodic similarity, pattern discovery, raga recognition, and tonic identification. CompMusic / ERC project at MTG, UPF.

title: CompMusic
subtitle: ERC research project on world music MIR
description: European Research Council funded project at MTG, UPF for computational analysis of non-Western music traditions. Contributed tonic identification, raga recognition, melodic similarity, and corpus tools for Hindustani and Carnatic music.

title: Dunya
subtitle: CompMusic music corpus explorer
description: Web platform for exploring and analyzing the CompMusic music corpora — large collections of annotated audio recordings across Hindustani, Carnatic, Makam, Beijing opera, and Andalusian musical traditions. Built at MTG, UPF.

title: KARMA / OpenMedEvalKit
subtitle: Open-source medical AI evaluation framework
description: An open-source framework for evaluating medical AI systems in Indian clinical contexts. Includes four domain-specific evaluation datasets covering ASR, document understanding, clinical NLP, and multilingual tasks. Built because most medical AI benchmarks don't reflect real Indian conditions.

title: BODHI
subtitle: Open clinical knowledge graphs for Indian healthcare AI
description: Bharat Ontology for Disease & Healthcare Informatics — open-source clinical knowledge graphs for grounding healthcare AI in verified clinical data. Covers 779 conditions, 4,037 symptom variants, 1,186 drugs, 812 lab tests, and 39 specialties. SNOMED-linked, production-tested. Available in Neo4j, CSV, PyTorch Geometric, and RDF formats.

title: EkaCare Public Models
subtitle: Parrotlet family on HuggingFace
description: Open-source release of EkaCare's Parrotlet model family on HuggingFace — medical ASR (Parrotlet-A), vision LLMs for medical documents (Parrotlet-V), and multilingual medical embeddings (Parrotlet-E). Purpose-built for Indian healthcare, available for research and clinical use.

title: EkaCare Medical Datasets
subtitle: Public clinical datasets on HuggingFace
description: Open evaluation datasets for medical AI in Indian healthcare contexts — covering ASR benchmarks, document understanding, multilingual clinical NLP, and structured extraction tasks. Released alongside KARMA and IndicMTEB to enable reproducible research.

title: Eka IndicMTEB
subtitle: Multilingual medical NLP benchmark for India
description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multilingual medical text embeddings across 22 Indian languages. Built to fill the gap in Indian healthcare NLP evaluation — MTEB and similar benchmarks are English-first and clinically naive.

title: Essentia
subtitle: Open-source audio analysis library
description: Contributed to Essentia during my PhD at MTG, UPF — an open-source C++/Python library for audio and music analysis. Now a standard tool in music information retrieval research, used widely across academia and industry.

title: pycompmusic
subtitle: Python tools for computational musicology
description: Python library developed as part of the CompMusic project at MTG, UPF — tools for accessing and processing the CompMusic music corpora, including audio feature extraction, metadata handling, and integration with the Dunya platform.

title: sms-tools
subtitle: Spectral modeling synthesis tools
description: Open-source software for sound analysis, transformation, and synthesis using spectral modeling. Contributed as part of the ASPMA Coursera MOOC at MTG, UPF — the course taught signal processing fundamentals to 100K+ students worldwide.

title: Melodic Analysis Datasets
subtitle: Annotated corpora for Indian classical music research
description: Open datasets released alongside my PhD thesis — annotated melodic data for Hindustani and Carnatic music, including tonic labels, raga annotations, and melodic similarity judgments. Widely used in subsequent MIR research on Indian art music.

title: Ragawise
subtitle: Raga recognition & visualization
description: Real-time raga recognition from audio using pitch class profiles and machine learning. Identifies the raga being performed and visualizes melodic patterns live. Built at HAMR 2015.

title: Sawaal-Jawaab
subtitle: Interactive Hindustani call-and-response
description: A musical game inspired by the Sawaal-Jawaab tradition in Indian classical music. Players sing or play a melodic phrase and the system responds with a melodic answer. Built at HAMR 2015.

title: Hodorizer
subtitle: Game of Thrones audio hack
description: Replaces all speech in any audio/video with Hodor's iconic vocabulary while preserving the original prosody, rhythm, and emotional tone using voice conversion techniques.

title: Music Spaghetti
subtitle: Infinite music loop generator
description: Essentia-powered implementation of the Infinite Jukebox concept — analyzes a song's beat structure to find perceptually similar segments and creates an endlessly recombining loop that always sounds musical.

title: YIN.js
subtitle: Pitch detection in the browser
description: JavaScript implementation of the YIN pitch detection algorithm for real-time fundamental frequency estimation — pitch tracking directly in the browser, no server needed.

title: Hindify
subtitle: Hindustani style transfer
description: Transforms Western pop songs into Hindustani classical style by applying raga-based melodic transformations — converting Western scales to raga scales and adding characteristic ornaments. Built at EchoNest Hack Day 2013.
